User Story: As an operator, I can start Orchestra with a WORKFLOW.md path and optional cockpit port so the daemon begins coordinating work.
Expected Behaviour: `orchestra <WORKFLOW.md> [--port N]` parses one workflow path, accepts `--port N` or `--port=N`, loads config, logs startup, starts the orchestrator, and starts the loopback cockpit when a port is supplied.
Edge Cases: Missing workflow path, malformed port, out-of-range port, port omitted, workflow load failure.
Validation Rules: Port must be integer 1..65535. Workflow path must be present. Workflow must parse before loop start.
Dependencies: Node 22+, Effect runtime, `loadWorkflow`, app layer, optional cockpit server.
Known Assumptions: The single daemon CLI is the intended current architecture. The old Ink dashboard is not supported.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/harness.test.ts`, `pnpm check`. Live: built daemon started with `node dist/cli/main.js <wf> --port <port>`.
Notes: Live smoke verified daemon startup and cockpit binding.

Feature Name: Workflow front matter parsing and defaults
User Story: As an adopter, I can describe orchestration in WORKFLOW.md front matter while relying on defaults for omitted blocks.
Expected Behaviour: Workflow content is split into YAML front matter plus trimmed Liquid body. Empty or missing front matter decodes to a complete `ServiceConfig`. Unknown keys are stripped. Path and env resolution runs after schema decode.
Edge Cases: No front matter, unterminated front matter, non-map front matter, invalid YAML, invalid scalar types, missing env var references.
Validation Rules: Positive integer schema for numeric knobs. `$VAR` resolves to env or becomes absent for optional secrets. Workspace path resolves to absolute.
Dependencies: `yaml`, Effect Schema, FileSystem, process env, workflow path directory.
Known Assumptions: Unknown keys are intentionally ignored for forward compatibility.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/workflow.test.ts`, `test/domain.test.ts`, `test/example-workflow.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Covers tracker, polling, workspace, hooks, agent, copilot, persistence, and budget defaults.

User Story: As a work author, I can write a Liquid issue prompt that renders from normalized issue and attempt context before the agent runs.
Expected Behaviour: Prompt renders with strict variables and strict filters. Issue fields, labels, blocker refs, and retry context are available. Unknown variables or filters fail with typed template errors.
Edge Cases: Missing optional fields, empty labels, empty blocker list, retry attempt present or absent, unknown Liquid variable, unknown filter.
Validation Rules: Liquid engine uses strict variables and filters. Errors map to `TemplateParseError` or `TemplateRenderError`.
Dependencies: `liquidjs`, domain `Issue`, `PromptScope`, workflow body.
Known Assumptions: Templates are trusted repo configuration, not untrusted web input.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/workflow.test.ts`, `test/example-workflow.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: No rendered prompt text is persisted by this QA artifact.

User Story: As an operator, I can point Orchestra at a GitHub repository so issues become candidate work and terminal issues can be reconciled.
Expected Behaviour: Adapter parses `owner/name`, creates Octokit with optional auth/baseUrl, fetches open issues for candidates, closed issues for terminal cleanup, and issue states by id for reconciliation. Pull requests are filtered out.
Edge Cases: Malformed repo, missing token, GitHub request failure, non-404 lookup failure, 404 vanished issue, enterprise endpoint override.
Validation Rules: Preflight requires `tracker.kind == github`, `repo`, and `api_key`. Adapter maps transport faults to typed tracker errors without logging secrets.
Dependencies: Octokit REST, resolved `ServiceConfig`, GitHub API.
Known Assumptions: Orchestra is a tracker reader. Agents perform tracker writes.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/tracker-github.test.ts`, preflight tests. Live: dummy token produced safe tracker warnings while cockpit stayed up.
Notes: Live smoke intentionally used dummy credentials and verified daemon resilience.

User Story: As the orchestrator, I need GitHub payloads normalized to one domain shape so downstream logic is tracker-independent.
Expected Behaviour: Issue number maps to id and identifier. Labels flatten and lowercase. Priority labels map to numeric priority. Body blocker refs are parsed. State derives from status labels with closed issues taking terminal precedence.
Edge Cases: Pull request payloads, null body, missing timestamps, closed issue with active label, not_planned close reason, repeated blockers, absent status labels.
Validation Rules: Domain Schema validates `Issue` and `IssueStateRef`. Closed GitHub state wins over active labels. Unknown blocker state remains null.
Dependencies: GitHub REST payload shape, workflow active and terminal states.
Known Assumptions: Blocker-state lookup is not implemented. Unknown blockers conservatively block Todo issues.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/tracker-github.test.ts`, `test/domain.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Normalization prevents GitHub-shaped data from leaking into core modules.

User Story: As an operator, I expect Orchestra to choose only eligible issues in deterministic priority order.
Expected Behaviour: Eligible issues are active, non-terminal, not already claimed, carry all required labels, and are not Todo issues with unresolved blockers. Selected issues sort by priority, created_at, then identifier.
Edge Cases: Terminal states, non-active states, already claimed issue, missing label, Todo with unknown blocker, In Progress with blocker, null priority/date.
Validation Rules: States and labels normalize. Terminal blockers are resolved. Null blocker state is unresolved. Sort tiebreak is total.
Dependencies: Domain `Issue`, tracker states/labels, claimed set.
Known Assumptions: Only Todo issues are blocked by unresolved blockers. In Progress is assumed accepted for work.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts` including property tests, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Selection itself never marks issues failed.

User Story: As an operator, I can cap global and per-state agent concurrency so the fleet does not overrun capacity.
Expected Behaviour: Dispatch planning admits sorted candidates while respecting global free slots and per-state caps. States without override use the global cap. Already running agents count against budgets.
Edge Cases: No slots, per-state cap lower than global, per-state key casing, more candidates than slots, transient negative availability.
Validation Rules: Concurrency values are positive integers. Free slots clamp at zero. State keys normalize.
Dependencies: Workflow agent config, running sessions by state, candidate list.
Known Assumptions: Greedy order is intended because candidates are already priority sorted.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts` concurrency cases and property tests, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Budget and operator pause can still reduce dispatch to zero.

User Story: As an agent runner, I get an isolated per-issue workspace with trusted lifecycle hooks executed safely around work.
Expected Behaviour: Workspace keys are sanitized, paths must stay under `workspace.root`, directories are created recursively, `after_create` runs only on first creation, configured hooks run in workspace cwd with timeout, output is truncated, and terminal workspaces can be cleaned.
Edge Cases: Identifier `..`, unusual characters, existing workspace, hook timeout, hook non-zero, missing root, failing before_remove, terminal cleanup missing dirs.
Validation Rules: Path-under-root invariant. Hook timeout positive. Hook output one-line truncated. `before_remove` failure is best-effort.
Dependencies: FileSystem, CommandExecutor, workflow workspace/hooks config.
Known Assumptions: Hook scripts are trusted repo/operator configuration.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/workspace-safety.test.ts`, `test/workspace-manager.test.ts`, `test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: No source workspace was touched during validation.

User Story: As Orchestra, I can run a headless Copilot turn in the issue workspace and stream normalized events back to the loop.
Expected Behaviour: Runner spawns configured Copilot command with prompt, JSON output, workspace `-C`, OS cwd pinned to workspace, auto tools, no color, generated or resumed session id, optional model, and GitHub token env. It emits `SessionStarted`, maps stdout, fails on terminal errors, and times out long turns.
Edge Cases: Command not found, malformed JSONL, missing result, non-zero result, input required, stdout read failure, timeout disabled, resume id present.
Validation Rules: `turn_timeout_ms > 0` enforces timeout. Missing successful result yields `AgentProcessExit`. Tokens are only passed via env and never logged.
Dependencies: GitHub Copilot CLI, CommandExecutor, `mapCopilotLine`, resolved tracker token.
Known Assumptions: Copilot owns remote session liveness. Resume is best-effort.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/agent-copilot.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Subprocess integration is covered with controlled test commands, not a live Copilot account.

User Story: As the orchestrator, I receive a stable internal event vocabulary regardless of Copilot transport details.
Expected Behaviour: Blank lines drop. Malformed lines emit `Malformed`. Assistant messages emit `AgentMessage` and non-empty `Notification`. Result success emits `TurnCompleted`. Result failure, session error, model failure, and input-required become typed terminal failures. Permission events surface auto-approval.
Edge Cases: Unparseable JSON, typeless object, missing content, missing usage fields, unknown event type, invalid timestamp.
Validation Rules: Mapper never throws. Usage maps numeric fields only. Empty type is malformed. Fallback timestamp is injected.
Dependencies: Copilot JSONL format, domain `AgentEvent`, typed errors.
Known Assumptions: Only operationally meaningful events are surfaced. Streaming deltas are intentionally omitted.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/agent-copilot.test.ts`, `test/domain.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Human summaries map by tag only to avoid payload leakage.

User Story: As an operator, I expect Orchestra to continuously reconcile, poll, select, dispatch, and process worker results through one state-owning fiber.
Expected Behaviour: On startup the loop restores state, cleans terminal workspaces, emits start, enqueues immediate tick, reconciles, preflights, fetches candidates, plans dispatch, starts scoped workers, receives agent events and completions over a mailbox, and persists mutations.
Edge Cases: Tracker error, preflight failure, worker failure, completion before max turns, completion at max turns, shutdown, command bus interruption.
Validation Rules: Only the owner fiber mutates orchestrator state. Workers communicate by queue messages. Failures map to observations and retry/completion state.
Dependencies: Effect Queue/Fiber/Scope, ports, durable store, observer tee.
Known Assumptions: Exactly-once is structural within one process. External tracker writes are agent-owned.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts`, `test/e2e-fake.test.ts`, `test/command-control.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Validated with fake tracker, fake agent, fake workspace, and TestClock.

User Story: As an operator, failed or incomplete work retries predictably without busy-looping or stranding issues.
Expected Behaviour: Clean completion below `max_turns` schedules short continuation retry. Failures schedule exponential backoff capped by `max_retry_backoff_ms`. Retry timers can fire and re-dispatch. Stale timeout command races are dropped.
Edge Cases: Attempt <= 0, very large attempts, cap below base, retry-now racing scheduled retry, max turns reached, retry issue no longer active.
Validation Rules: Failure delay is capped `10000 * 2^(attempt-1)`. Continuation delay is 1000ms. Retry claims occupy issue slot until cleared or released.
Dependencies: Clock/TestClock, retry entries, workflow agent config.
Known Assumptions: Retries are issue-level, not tracker-state transitions.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts`, `test/command-control.test.ts`, `test/orchestrator-loop.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Retry-now exactly-once race is covered.

User Story: As an operator, Orchestra stops or updates sessions when tracker state changes or an agent stalls.
Expected Behaviour: Each tick checks stalls before tracker state. Stalled workers are killed and retried. Terminal issues are killed, cleaned, and marked completed. Active issues update refs. Vanished or other-state issues are released without cleanup. Retrying issues reconcile by tracker state only.
Edge Cases: Stall timeout <= 0, tracker refresh failure, terminal retrying issue, vanished running issue, active issue with updated labels, stall and terminal both true.
Validation Rules: Stall detection uses monotonic last-event time and only runs when timeout > 0. Refresh failure leaves workers and retries untouched.
Dependencies: IssueTracker, worker registry, workspace manager, active/terminal states.
Known Assumptions: Terminal cleanup assumes terminal identifiers can be derived from tracker payloads.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/orchestrator-pure.test.ts`, `test/restore-reconcile.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Stall takes precedence by design.

User Story: As an operator, I can cap cumulative token spend so new dispatch pauses when the ceiling is reached.
Expected Behaviour: Absent budget is inert. Configured budget projects limit/spent/remaining/paused and withholds new dispatch when total tokens are at or over ceiling. In-flight work, retries, and reconciliation continue.
Edge Cases: No budget block, empty budget block, exactly at ceiling, over ceiling, budget raised or cleared by settings reload.
Validation Rules: Budget ceiling must be positive integer. Remaining clamps at zero. Paused is pure from totals and config.
Dependencies: Agent usage totals, workflow budget config, LiveBudget mirror.
Known Assumptions: Spend only grows. Resume usually comes from config change.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/budget-pure.test.ts`, `test/budget-gate.test.ts`, `test/cross-feature.test.ts`. Live: state included budget block.
Notes: Budget pause and operator pause are separate.

User Story: As an operator, I can restart the daemon without losing authoritative bookkeeping.
Expected Behaviour: State writes to `<workspace.root>/.orchestra/state.json` through a debounced writer, atomic temp+rename, restrictive POSIX modes, final flush on shutdown, versioned Schema JSON, and corruption rename-aside with clean start.
Edge Cases: Missing file, corrupt JSON, IO write failure, unencodable `agent_rate_limits`, debounce/final-flush race, Windows mode behavior.
Validation Rules: Checkpoint decodes via versioned Schema. Only `agent_rate_limits` can degrade to null on encode fault. State dir mode 0700 and file mode 0600 on POSIX.
Dependencies: FileSystem, Effect Scope, persistence config, store decorator.
Known Assumptions: Persistence failures are logged and do not crash teardown.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/persistence.test.ts`, `test/restore-reconcile.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: Throwaway live workflow used temp paths.

Feature Name: Restart restore and optional session resume
User Story: As an operator, a daemon restart re-derives in-flight work safely and can optionally resume Copilot sessions.
Expected Behaviour: Completed history, totals, and rate limits restore. Orphaned running issues become due-immediately continuation retries. Retry timers re-arm from wall-clock. Terminal/vanished issues reconcile before dispatch. `persistence.resume_sessions` controls whether session ids are passed to continuation dispatch.
Edge Cases: Cold start, orphaned terminal, orphaned vanished, retry due in past/future, resume disabled, resume enabled but stale session fails.
Validation Rules: Persisted state version must decode. Resume defaults false. Stale resume self-heals by retrying fresh.
Dependencies: Persistence, IssueTracker, AgentRunner, Clock, RestoreStatus.
Known Assumptions: Workspace on disk is the true progress record. Remote Copilot session continuity is not guaranteed.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/restore-reconcile.test.ts`, `test/domain.test.ts`, `pnpm check`.
Notes: No live Copilot session resume was attempted.

User Story: As an operator, I can understand daemon activity through structured logs and cockpit state without unsafe payloads.
Expected Behaviour: Observations format as one logfmt line with stable tags and status glyph vocabulary. Recent events, live activity, completions, budget, restore, rate limits, and control project additively into snapshots. Agent summaries are friendly and never echo payload text.
Edge Cases: Unknown event tag, long messages, high-volume agent events, cold start restore absent, missing optional snapshot blocks, no-color env.
Validation Rules: Truncation caps one-line fields. Phase mapping is exhaustive. Event rings are bounded. Additive fields omit when absent.
Dependencies: ObserverLive, observer tee, RecentEvents, LiveActivity, RecentCompletions, snapshot projection.
Known Assumptions: Snapshot v1 remains additive and older readers tolerate omitted fields.
Test Cases: Automated: glyph, observer, recent event, live activity, snapshot, and cross-feature tests. Live: state keys verified.
Notes: Live smoke verified `/api/v1/state` top-level keys.

User Story: As a cockpit user or local tool, I can read live fleet state without a token on loopback.
Expected Behaviour: Endpoint returns `JSON.stringify(toSnapshot(...))` bytes for current store plus additive rings/status blocks. It is token-free but guarded by loopback Host check at server chokepoint.
Edge Cases: Non-loopback Host, missing optional blocks, configured/unconfigured budget, no SPA build, tracker down while daemon up.
Validation Rules: Read group has no bearer middleware. Server rejects non-loopback Host. Byte-compatible test pins serialization.
Dependencies: Cockpit HttpApi, OrchestratorStore, observability services, LiveBudget, ControlStatus, RestoreStatus.
Known Assumptions: Loopback local read access is acceptable for this local operator tool.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/cockpit-server.test.ts`. Live curl: 200 token-free. Browser smoke loaded Fleet from state.
Notes: Live state keys included counts, running, retrying, completed, totals, budget, events.

Feature Name: Static cockpit serving and token bootstrap
User Story: As an operator, I can open the cockpit in a browser and the SPA can authenticate mutations.
Expected Behaviour: Server serves built `dist/cockpit` assets, falls back to token-injected `index.html`, escapes token content against script breakout, and returns graceful 404 when assets are absent.
Edge Cases: Missing build dir, SPA route fallback, traversal attempt, malformed percent escape, token contains script terminator, built HTML should not contain token.
Validation Rules: Asset paths resolve under static root or return null. Host must be loopback. Only served index bytes are rewritten.
Dependencies: Vite build output, CockpitToken, FileSystem, static handler.
Known Assumptions: Token is a per-process local operator secret, not a web account credential.
Test Cases: Automated: static, security, and server tests. Live curl: root 200 with injected token. Browser: tokenInjected=true.
Notes: Production build emitted assets before smoke.

User Story: As an operator, I can inspect only safe editable workflow knobs in the cockpit settings form.
Expected Behaviour: Endpoint returns whitelisted raw-front-matter settings: polling interval, agent concurrency/turn/backoff knobs, per-state caps, and budget ceiling. It never returns tracker, repo, api_key, hooks, workspace, copilot, persistence, or prompt body.
Edge Cases: Missing front matter, invalid workflow file, absent budget ceiling, empty per-state map, raw `$VAR` outside whitelist.
Validation Rules: Editable projection contains only `polling`, `agent`, and `budget`.
Dependencies: WorkflowFile service, FileSystem, ServiceConfig schema.
Known Assumptions: Token-free settings read is acceptable because it is loopback and secret-free.
Test Cases: Automated: settings tests. Live curl: keys were `polling,agent,budget`. Browser: no tracker/API-key text.
Notes: Settings read includes no secret-bearing keys.

User Story: As an operator, I can change safe live knobs from the cockpit and have them apply without disrupting in-flight work.
Expected Behaviour: Typed sparse patch is validated, staged into a temp WORKFLOW.md, resolved, sent through `ReloadConfig`, and atomically committed only after owner fiber ack. Scalar existing-key edits preserve all other bytes. Structural edits are best-effort with compact arrays and empty parent pruning.
Edge Cases: Negative values, no-op patch, clear budget with null, overlapping writes, gate timeout, missing workflow file, scalar edit with aligned comments, structural comment normalization.
Validation Rules: All numeric values are positive integers except blank/null budget clears ceiling. Patch schema is the HTTP boundary. Write is validate, gate, rename.
Dependencies: WorkflowFile, CommandBus, FileSystem, LiveBudget, owner-fiber config ref.
Known Assumptions: Only whitelisted knobs are hot-applicable. Structural config still requires restart or manual edit.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/settings.test.ts`, `test/cockpit-settings.test.ts`, server tests, `pnpm check`.
Notes: DEF-001 fixed: invalid-patch test used `as any` and stale Biome suppression. It now decodes unknown payload through `SettingsPatch`.

User Story: As an operator, I can pause new dispatch from Fleet or Settings without killing running work.
Expected Behaviour: Authorized pause sends `PauseDispatch` through CommandBus, owner fiber sets operator latch, snapshot control shows paused by operator, and in-flight workers/retries/reconcile continue.
Edge Cases: Already paused, no running sessions, budget also paused, command bus interrupted, no token, cross-origin request.
Validation Rules: Mutation requires bearer token plus loopback Origin/Host. Command result is acked by owner fiber or service unavailable.
Dependencies: Cockpit API client/server, CommandBus, ControlStatus, dispatch gate.
Known Assumptions: Pause affects new dispatch only and is runtime-only.
Test Cases: Automated: command-control, pause-control, client tests. Live curl: 401 no token, 403 cross-origin, 200 authorized. Browser saw Dispatch control.
Notes: Fleet now exposes dispatch control.

User Story: As an operator, I can resume operator-paused dispatch while budget pauses remain respected.
Expected Behaviour: Authorized resume clears only operator latch. Snapshot control is omitted or clear when nothing pauses dispatch. Budget pauses continue to hide resume toggle and require budget change.
Edge Cases: Not paused, paused by budget, command bus interrupted, stale cockpit poll, no token, cross-origin request.
Validation Rules: Same auth as all mutations. UI derives budget pause as non-clearable. Dispatch still subject to budget after resume.
Dependencies: CommandBus, ControlStatus, BudgetStatus, API client.
Known Assumptions: Resume cannot clear a budget pause by design.
Test Cases: Automated: command-control and pause-control tests. Live curl authorized resume returned unpaused state.
Notes: Pause and resume verified against built daemon.

User Story: As an operator, I can fire a pending retry immediately from Kanban when I want to accelerate a backing-off issue.
Expected Behaviour: Authorized retry-now accepts a retrying issue, removes or re-arms pending backoff, and dispatches exactly once if eligible. Unknown or ineligible ids return accepted false with reason.
Edge Cases: Unknown id, issue no longer retrying, scheduled RetryDue race, tracker now terminal, no token, encoded slash/space in id.
Validation Rules: Client URL-encodes issue id. Owner fiber serializes command and race. Mutation auth required.
Dependencies: CommandBus, retry map, IssueTracker, API client, Kanban cards.
Known Assumptions: Retry-now does not move tracker state.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/command-control.test.ts`, `test/cockpit-client.test.ts`, `test/cockpit-kanban.test.ts`.
Notes: Live smoke did not create a retrying issue. Automated tests cover behavior and race.

User Story: As an operator, I can cancel a specific running session from Kanban without affecting other workers.
Expected Behaviour: Authorized cancel interrupts only named worker, releases registry/claim state, emits cancellation observation, and returns accepted true. Unknown or non-running ids return accepted false with reason.
Edge Cases: Unknown id, issue retrying not running, multiple workers active, repeated cancel, stale card, no token, cross-origin request.
Validation Rules: Client URL-encodes id. UI requires inline confirm. Mutation auth required. Owner fiber serializes state mutation.
Dependencies: CommandBus, worker registry, API client, Kanban view.
Known Assumptions: Cancel stops an Orchestra worker and does not write tracker state.
Test Cases: Automated: command-control, client, and Kanban tests. Browser smoke verified Kanban route and columns.
Notes: Kanban cancel has 4s two-click confirm window.

User Story: As the SPA, I can call cockpit endpoints through a typed fetch wrapper and show useful errors to operators.
Expected Behaviour: GET calls are token-free. POST/PUT attach bearer token when present. JSON bodies set content type. Issue ids encode in paths. Non-2xx and network failures become `ApiError`. `describeError` maps common codes to guidance.
Edge Cases: No token, wrong token, network failure, 503 owner timeout, empty response, server repeats guidance.
Validation Rules: Only mutating verbs carry auth. Error code/message/detail are stable. Server detail is appended only when useful.
Dependencies: Browser fetch, injected `window.__ORCHESTRA_COCKPIT_TOKEN__`, API wire types.
Known Assumptions: Client is DOM-free except optional token global access, enabling Node tests.
Test Cases: Automated: client and error tests. Live curl and browser smoke.
Notes: Browser smoke confirmed token bootstrap visible to SPA.

User Story: As a cockpit user, I see fresh data when available and stale-but-present data when polling fails.
Expected Behaviour: Poller starts immediately, never overlaps requests, retains last successful value, flips connecting to live, flips to stale after post-success failures, stays connecting on initial failure, and stops cleanly.
Edge Cases: Slow fetch, rejected fetch, first poll failure, unmount with in-flight request, app interval fixed.
Validation Rules: Next timeout schedules only after fetch settles. Stopped poller ignores late results.
Dependencies: Browser timers, API client, React `usePolling`.
Known Assumptions: `COCKPIT_POLL_MS` is fixed at 2000ms.
Test Cases: Automated: `test/cockpit-poller.test.ts`. Browser smoke used real polling.
Notes: No overlap covered by unit test.

Feature Name: App shell routing and keyboard navigation
User Story: As a cockpit user, I can move among Fleet, Kanban, Events, and Settings with links or `g` shortcuts.
Expected Behaviour: Hash routes parse case-insensitively. Unknown or empty routes default to Fleet. Nav links update hash without full reload. Active link gets `aria-current`. Shortcuts use `g` then route key within 1200ms and do not hijack editable fields.
Edge Cases: Unknown hash, modified click, expired prefix, route key while input focused, mobile nav overflow.
Validation Rules: Routes are limited to fleet, kanban, events, settings. Shortcuts derive from route map.
Dependencies: React, hashchange listener, AppShell, useKeyboardShortcuts.
Known Assumptions: No command palette is implemented. Current shortcut scope is navigation only.
Test Cases: Automated: router/design tests. Browser smoke clicked routes at desktop and verified mobile nav links.
Notes: Browser smoke verified `#/kanban`, `#/events`, and `#/settings`.

User Story: As an operator, I can inspect fleet health, running sessions, spend, restore state, rate limits, and dispatch control from the default screen.
Expected Behaviour: Fleet polls state, shows connection banner, dispatch control, metrics, running sessions table, totals, and optional budget/restore/rate-limit panels.
Edge Cases: No first snapshot, no running sessions, unknown phase, unparseable times, absent additive blocks, long workspace path, stale poll.
Validation Rules: Unknown phases show honest drift badge. Times use sentinel instead of fake zero. Tables scroll horizontally when needed.
Dependencies: `toFleetView`, Poller, StatusChip, DispatchControl, snapshot wire.
Known Assumptions: Fleet is default cockpit route.
Test Cases: Automated: fleet and cross-feature tests. Browser smoke verified Fleet loaded with no overflow.
Notes: First-snapshot placeholder now tells operator to start daemon if needed.

User Story: As an operator, I can view issues by Orchestra state and act on running/retrying work without implying tracker drag/drop.
Expected Behaviour: Kanban derives Claimed, Running, Retrying, Completed columns. Claimed is count-only. Running cards expose confirmed Cancel. Retrying cards expose Retry now. Completed cards are non-actionable. Action state keys by session instance.
Edge Cases: Claimed count lower than running plus retrying, empty columns, completed rich block absent, action rejected, action throws, issue reappears with new started_at.
Validation Rules: Pending claimed count clamps at zero. Action-state map prunes stale keys. Cancel requires second click within 4000ms.
Dependencies: `toKanban`, API client, StatusChip, snapshot counts/running/retrying/completed.
Known Assumptions: Drag/drop is unsupported because Orchestra does not write tracker state.
Test Cases: Automated: Kanban tests. Browser smoke verified four columns.
Notes: Claimed count-only limitation is documented in UI and docs.

User Story: As an operator, I can review recent daemon events and filter them quickly.
Expected Behaviour: Events polls state, reverses append-only recent events to newest-first, shows glyph/time/kind/identifier/message, and filters by level, kind, and free-text query.
Edge Cases: No events, all filtered out, unparseable timestamp, missing identifier, many event kinds.
Validation Rules: Level filter is all/info/warn. Kind all bypasses kind filter. Search covers message and identifier.
Dependencies: `toEventsView`, `filterEvents`, RecentEvents snapshot field, Poller.
Known Assumptions: Events ring is bounded and not a forensic log.
Test Cases: Automated: events tests. Browser smoke verified three filter controls and no overflow.
Notes: Dummy-token live run produced tracker_error events; route smoke focused on UI structure.

User Story: As an operator, I can edit safe live knobs and understand each knob without exposing secrets.
Expected Behaviour: Settings loads editable settings, renders numeric controlled inputs with hints, validates before save, sends sparse patches, updates baseline after successful save, shows saved/error messages, and embeds DispatchControl. It never renders tracker/API-key data.
Edge Cases: Settings load failure, save failure, invalid number, blank budget ceiling, no per-state caps, no-op save, budget-paused control.
Validation Rules: All numeric fields must be whole numbers > 0 except blank max total tokens clears budget. `aria-invalid` and alerts surface errors.
Dependencies: GET/PUT settings endpoints, `validateSettings`, API client, DispatchControl.
Known Assumptions: Adding new per-state rows is not implemented. Existing rows are editable when present.
Test Cases: Automated: cockpit-settings and workflow-file tests. Browser smoke verified five numeric fields and no tracker/API-key text.
Notes: Save button is disabled while invalid or saving.

Feature Name: Cockpit design, accessibility, and responsive behavior
User Story: As an operator, I can use the cockpit on dark screens, high-contrast preferences, reduced-motion preferences, desktop, and narrow mobile widths.
Expected Behaviour: Cockpit uses shared CSS tokens for statuses, surfaces, text, borders, focus, motion, spacing, and mono/sans typography. Color is never the only status signal. Nav wraps. Tables scroll. Narrow viewport adjusts padding/nav. Controls use semantic elements.
Edge Cases: Low contrast, reduced motion, narrow viewport, long tables, status chip duplication, destructive action misclick.
Validation Rules: Default text and border contrast is raised. `prefers-contrast` reinforces further. `prefers-reduced-motion` disables transitions. Controls are real buttons, inputs, selects, and links.
Dependencies: CSS tokens, glyph vocabulary, React views.
Known Assumptions: No shadcn components are installed. Source-owned primitives are the active design system.
Test Cases: Automated: cockpit-design tests. Browser smoke desktop 1280 and mobile 390 with no horizontal overflow.
Notes: Dirty working-tree changes remediate the earlier contrast/responsive/hierarchy critique.

User Story: As an operator, I get clear failures for unsupported or incomplete runtime config before dispatch starts.
Expected Behaviour: Preflight rejects unsupported tracker kind, missing GitHub repo, and missing API key. Errors are typed with unique tags. CLI, workflow, tracker, agent, and workspace failures use discriminable classes and avoid secret payloads.
Edge Cases: Missing kind, unsupported kind, missing repo, missing API key, structural equality, exhaustive switch over union.
Validation Rules: Preflight validates support/presence after lenient config decode. Every sample error tag is unique.
Dependencies: ServiceConfig, error classes, Effect error channel.
Known Assumptions: Workflow decode stays lenient. Preflight enforces dispatch readiness.
Test Cases: Automated: preflight and error tests. Live dummy token produced safe structured tracker error.
Notes: No secret values were reproduced in this inventory.

User Story: As a maintainer, I can produce a runnable daemon bundle and served cockpit assets with one build command.
Expected Behaviour: `pnpm build` runs `tsup` for `dist/cli/main.js` and `vite build` for `dist/cockpit/index.html` plus hashed assets. The daemon locates the built cockpit relative to bundle and serves it on `--port`.
Edge Cases: Missing dist cockpit, source maps, static route fallback, asset hash changes, build output cleanup.
Validation Rules: Typecheck/lint/test should pass before build. Vite output root is `dist/cockpit`. tsup target is Node 22.
Dependencies: tsup, Vite, React plugin, package scripts, dist directory.
Known Assumptions: Package is private and was not published during this loop.
Test Cases: Automated/build: `pnpm check && pnpm build`. Live: built daemon served API and SPA. Browser: served SPA loaded.
Notes: Build emitted CLI bundle, cockpit HTML, CSS, JS, and source maps.

User Story: As a maintainer, I can require every change to keep React Doctor at 100/100 so React code quality regressions fail locally and in CI.
Expected Behaviour: `pnpm doctor:score` runs the pinned React Doctor scanner and fails unless the JSON report has score 100 and zero diagnostics. `pnpm check` includes that score gate. CI also runs the local score gate, while the dedicated React Doctor workflow scans the full project with warning-level blocking and fails when the action score output is not exactly 100.
Edge Cases: Score API unavailable or empty, React Doctor diagnostics, full-scope PR scan, pnpm policy exceptions, Vite HTML entry reachability false positive.
Validation Rules: Score must equal numeric 100 and total diagnostics must equal 0. GitHub Action score output must be `100`. React Doctor version is pinned to 0.5.8. Workflow uses `blocking: warning` and `scope: full`.
Dependencies: `react-doctor`, `doctor.config.json`, `scripts/check-react-doctor.mjs`, GitHub Actions, pnpm, React/Vite cockpit.
Known Assumptions: React Doctor 0.5.8 does not follow the Vite `src/cockpit/index.html` entry into `src/cockpit/main.tsx`, so only `deslop/unused-file` is ignored for `src/cockpit/**`; every other cockpit diagnostic still counts.
Test Cases: Automated: `pnpm doctor:score`, `pnpm check`, React Doctor workflow config. Live: built daemon smoke after gate changes.
Notes: Frozen install passes with pnpm release-age and trust policies active. React Doctor reports 100/100 locally.
